product_overview: NewsLens UI is a news analysis web app that helps users search for trending topics and explore static analytics dashboards. It presents mock news insights, sentiment distribution, and article feeds in a simple Next.js interface.
core_goals: Search for a news topic and review analysis settings
Explore trending topics to find relevant news themes
View analytics summaries and sentiment distribution for news data
Browse news feed articles and open more details for an article

features.name: NewsLens Search
features.description: Allows users to enter a topic and refine the analysis using time horizon and volume depth filters before viewing search results.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Fill in the search input field with a topic -> Select a time horizon filter -> Select a volume depth filter -> Start analysis -> See the analyzed search results
Navigate to / -> Open the trending topics list -> Choose a trending topic -> Start analysis -> See the analyzed search results
Navigate to / -> Fill in the search input field with a topic -> Start analysis -> See the analyzed search results

features.name: Analysis Dashboard
features.description: Shows the overview of article volume, sentiment distribution, and a news feed so users can review the current analysis.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> View the analytics summary -> Review the sentiment distribution -> Browse the news feed articles -> Open more details for an article
Navigate to /dashboard -> Use sidebar navigation -> Move to another dashboard section -> View the updated dashboard content

features.name: NewsLens Search
features.description: Search interface with filters for topic, time horizon, and volume depth

features.name: Analysis Dashboard
features.description: Dashboard displaying total articles, sentiment distribution, and news feed
user_interactions: Click on Read More for articles

known_limitations.issue: All data is static mock data, not connected to backend yet
known_limitations.impact: Interactions do not trigger real API calls or state updates
